You are a blind ceiling-quality judge. You receive one task and three anonymized candidate patches. Do not infer or mention model, arm, attempt, harness, or author identity; judge only the task text and diffs.

Task text:
Add support for item_comments to syndication framework
Description
	
Add comments argument to feed.add_item() in syndication.views so that item_comments can be defined directly without having to take the detour via item_extra_kwargs .
Additionally, comments is already explicitly mentioned in the feedparser, but not implemented in the view.

P1 patch.diff:
```diff
diff --git a/django/contrib/syndication/views.py b/django/contrib/syndication/views.py
index 68ffb0bfe0..a531fcfc3a 100644
--- a/django/contrib/syndication/views.py
+++ b/django/contrib/syndication/views.py
@@ -199,10 +199,17 @@ class Feed:
             if updateddate and is_naive(updateddate):
                 updateddate = make_aware(updateddate, tz)
 
+            item_extra_kwargs = dict(self.item_extra_kwargs(item))
+            if 'comments' in item_extra_kwargs:
+                comments = item_extra_kwargs.pop('comments')
+            else:
+                comments = self._get_dynamic_attr('item_comments', item)
+
             feed.add_item(
                 title=title,
                 link=link,
                 description=description,
+                comments=comments,
                 unique_id=self._get_dynamic_attr('item_guid', item, link),
                 unique_id_is_permalink=self._get_dynamic_attr(
                     'item_guid_is_permalink', item),
@@ -214,6 +221,6 @@ class Feed:
                 author_link=author_link,
                 categories=self._get_dynamic_attr('item_categories', item),
                 item_copyright=self._get_dynamic_attr('item_copyright', item),
-                **self.item_extra_kwargs(item)
+                **item_extra_kwargs
             )
         return feed

index 13aa944e8a..993f1e2ff8 100644
--- a/docs/ref/contrib/syndication.txt
+++ b/docs/ref/contrib/syndication.txt
@@ -673,6 +673,22 @@ This example illustrates all possible attributes and methods for a
             Returns the URL for every item in the feed.
             """
 
+        # ITEM COMMENTS URL -- One of the following three is optional. This is
+        # only applicable to RSS feeds.
+
+        def item_comments(self, item):
+            """
+            Takes an item, as returned by items(), and returns the item's
+            comments URL as a normal Python string.
+            """
+
+        def item_comments(self):
+            """
+            Returns the comments URL for every item in the feed.
+            """
+
+        item_comments = 'https://www.example.com/comments/' # Hard-coded comments URL.
+
         # ITEM_GUID -- The following method is optional. If not provided, the
         # item's link is used by default.

+class TestRss2FeedWithComments(TestRss2Feed):

+        return 'http://example.com/blog/%s/comments/' % item.pk

+        }

     def test_rss2_single_enclosure(self):
```

Ties are allowed only by putting indistinguishable packets in the same tier. For every strict winner-loser pair created by tiers, include exactly one concrete one-sentence delta citing something visible in the diffs. Output ONLY the JSON object -- no prose before or after, no markdown fences.

strict_win_deltas.delta: P1 adds an explicit `comments=comments` argument to `feed.add_item()` after resolving `item_comments`, while P2 hides the new hook by injecting `'comments'` into `item_extra_kwargs` before `**item_extra_kwargs`.

P1 reconciles the new direct `item_comments` hook with the existing `'comments'` detour by popping that key from `item_extra_kwargs`, while P3 adds `comments=...` directly before `**self.item_extra_kwargs(item)`.

P2 preserves existing `item_extra_kwargs` comments values by checking `'comments' not in item_extra_kwargs`, while P3 always passes `comments=self._get_dynamic_attr(...)` alongside `**self.item_extra_kwargs(item)`.
P1 adds a dedicated regression test for `TestRss2FeedWithCommentsFromExtraKwargs`, while P2 only tests the new `item_comments` hook path.

P1 avoids duplicate keyword errors by removing `'comments'` from the copied extra kwargs before passing `comments=comments`, while P3 would collide when `item_extra_kwargs()` returns a `comments` key.
P2 checks for an existing `'comments'` key before adding the dynamic hook value, while P3 unconditionally combines `comments=...` with arbitrary `**self.item_extra_kwargs(item)`.
P1 documents that `item_comments` is only applicable to RSS feeds, while P2's new documentation describes the comments URL hook without that RSS-only constraint.
P1 keeps the existing `item_extra_kwargs` comments detour working and tests it, while P3's direct keyword addition breaks that explicitly mentioned migration path.
P2 uses a realistic per-item comments URL in its test feed, while P3 sets `item_comments` to the unrelated copyright string `'Copyright (c) 2007, Sally Smith'`.
P1 makes `comments` a visible first-class `feed.add_item()` argument in `views.py`, while P2 makes future readers discover it indirectly inside the constructed `item_extra_kwargs` mapping.
P1 separates compatibility handling from the new API by copying and popping `item_extra_kwargs`, while P3 leaves the old and new APIs competing for the same `comments` keyword.
P2 centralizes precedence by only adding the hook value when extras lack `comments`, while P3 provides no precedence rule for users who already supply `comments` through `item_extra_kwargs()`.

Task text:
Add support for item_comments to syndication framework
Description
	
Add comments argument to feed.add_item() in syndication.views so that item_comments can be defined directly without having to take the detour via item_extra_kwargs .
Additionally, comments is already explicitly mentioned in the feedparser, but not implemented in the view.

P1 patch.diff:
```diff
diff --git a/django/contrib/syndication/views.py b/django/contrib/syndication/views.py
index 68ffb0bfe0..a531fcfc3a 100644
--- a/django/contrib/syndication/views.py
+++ b/django/contrib/syndication/views.py
@@ -199,10 +199,17 @@ class Feed:
             if updateddate and is_naive(updateddate):
                 updateddate = make_aware(updateddate, tz)
 
+            item_extra_kwargs = dict(self.item_extra_kwargs(item))
+            if 'comments' in item_extra_kwargs:
+                comments = item_extra_kwargs.pop('comments')
+            else:
+                comments = self._get_dynamic_attr('item_comments', item)
+
             feed.add_item(
                 title=title,
                 link=link,
                 description=description,
+                comments=comments,
                 unique_id=self._get_dynamic_attr('item_guid', item, link),
```
